actions.name: ASK_USER_QUESTION
actions.description: Broadcast 1-4 structured questions back to the user. Each question has a short header, a full question string, and optional multi-choice options with descriptions and previews. This is a structured-question broadcast surface — the action returns the question payload as data so a UI layer can render it; the action does NOT block waiting for an answer. UI integration is pending; for now treat the response as a published question, not as an interactive prompt.

parameters.name: questions
parameters.description: Array of 1-4 question objects. Each: { question: string, header: string, options?: Array<{label, description?, preview?}>, multiSelect?: boolean }. If options is empty/undefined, the question is treated as freeform.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Broadcast 1-4 structured questions to the user (UI integration pending; non-blocking).

exampleCalls.user: Use ASK_USER_QUESTION with the provided parameters.

actions.name: BASH
actions.description: Execute a shell command via /bin/bash -c <command>. Runs synchronously in the session cwd by default. Returns stdout, stderr, and exit code. Hard timeout kills the command. Paths under the configured blocklist are off-limits as cwd.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: Shell command to run; executed via /bin/bash -c <command>.

parameters.name: description
parameters.description: Five to ten word humanly-readable summary of the command.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Hard timeout in ms; clamped to [100, 600000]. Default 120000.

parameters.name: cwd
parameters.description: Absolute working directory; must not resolve under a blocked path. Defaults to the session cwd.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Run a shell command synchronously.

exampleCalls.user: Use BASH with the provided parameters.

actions.name: BROWSER
actions.description: Single BROWSER action — control whichever browser target is registered. Targets are pluggable: `workspace` (electrobun-embedded BrowserView, the default; falls back to a JSDOM web mode when the desktop bridge isn't configured), `bridge` (the user's real Chrome/Safari via the Agent Browser Bridge companion extension), and `computeruse` (a local puppeteer-driven Chromium via plugin-computeruse). The agent uses what is available — the BrowserService picks the active target when none is specified. Use `subaction: "autofill-login"` with `domain` (and optional `username`, `submit`) to vault-gated autofill into an open workspace tab.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Compatibility alias for subaction from older BROWSER_ACTION calls. Prefer subaction in new plans.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Browser action to perform

parameters.name: tabAction
parameters.description: Tab operation when subaction is tab

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: Required when subaction is autofill-login: registrable hostname (e.g. `github.com`).

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: When using autofill-login: specific saved login; omit for most recently modified.

parameters.name: submit
parameters.description: When using autofill-login: submit the form after filling (default false).

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Session or tab id to target

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL for open or navigate

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: Selector for click, type, or wait

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text for type

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Keyboard key for press

parameters.name: pixels
parameters.description: Scroll distance in pixels

parameters.name: timeoutMs
parameters.description: Command timeout in milliseconds

parameters.name: script
parameters.description: Script for eval

parameters.name: watchMode
parameters.description: Hint that the user is watching; prefers realistic-* subactions for click/fill so the cursor moves visibly and pointer events fire faithfully.

parameters.name: cursorDurationMs
parameters.description: Cursor animation duration (ms) for realistic-* subactions

parameters.name: perCharDelayMs
parameters.description: Per-character delay for realistic-type/realistic-fill (ms)

parameters.name: replace
parameters.description: Replace existing input value when filling (vs append) — applies to realistic-fill

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Cursor target X (CSS pixels) for cursor-move

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Cursor target Y (CSS pixels) for cursor-move

actions.descriptionCompressed: Browser tab/page control: open/navigate/click/type/screenshot/state; subaction autofill-login + domain autofill vault-gated credential into workspace tab pre-authorized in Settings Vault Logins. Bridge settings/status use MANAGE_BROWSER_BRIDGE.

exampleCalls.user: Use BROWSER with the provided parameters.

actions.name: CLEAR_LINEAR_ACTIVITY
actions.description: Clear the Linear activity log
actions.descriptionCompressed: clear Linear activity log

actions.name: COMPUTER_USE
actions.description:
computer_use:
  purpose: Canonical cross-platform computer-use action for real desktop interaction on macOS, Linux, and Windows when direct computer operation is required.
  guidance: Take a screenshot before acting. After each desktop action, the result includes a screenshot when available. Use this standard plugin action, not a LifeOps wrapper, for Finder/Desktop/native-app/browser/file/terminal workflows on the owner's machine.
  actions: screenshot/click/click_with_modifiers/double_click/right_click/mouse_move/type/key/key_combo/scroll/drag/detect_elements/ocr.

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Desktop action to perform.

parameters.name: coordinate
parameters.description: Target [x, y] pixel coordinate.

parameters.name: startCoordinate
parameters.description: Start [x, y] pixel coordinate for drag.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text to type.

parameters.name: modifiers
parameters.description: Modifier keys to hold during click_with_modifiers, e.g. ['cmd', 'shift'] or ['ctrl'].

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Single key or combo string depending on action.

parameters.name: button
parameters.description: Mouse button for click_with_modifiers.

parameters.name: clicks
parameters.description: Number of clicks for click_with_modifiers.

parameters.name: scrollDirection
parameters.description: Scroll direction.

parameters.name: scrollAmount
parameters.description: Scroll tick count.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Canonical cross-platform desktop control: screenshot/click/modified click/double/right/move/type/key/key_combo/scroll/drag/detect_elements/ocr.

exampleCalls.user: Use COMPUTER_USE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: DESKTOP
actions.description: Single DESKTOP action — dispatches local desktop operations through the computer-use service.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Desktop operation group. Reserved future values: screenshot, ocr, detect_elements (currently on COMPUTER_USE).

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Sub-op verb for the chosen op (e.g. read/write for file, list/focus for window, execute for terminal).

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Primary file or directory path (file op).

parameters.name: filepath
parameters.description: Upstream alias for path (file op).

parameters.name: dirpath
parameters.description: Upstream alias for directory path (file op).

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Content for write, append, or upload (file op).

parameters.name: encoding
parameters.description: Encoding for read/download (file op).

parameters.name: oldText
parameters.description: Replacement source text for edit (file op).

parameters.name: newText
parameters.description: Replacement destination text for edit (file op).

parameters.name: old_text
parameters.description: Upstream edit source text (file op).

parameters.name: new_text
parameters.description: Upstream edit destination text (file op).

parameters.name: find
parameters.description: Upstream alias for old_text (file op).

parameters.name: replace
parameters.description: Upstream alias for new_text (file op).

parameters.name: windowId
parameters.description: Window identifier (window op).

parameters.name: windowTitle
parameters.description: Window title or app-name query (window op).

parameters.name: arrangement
parameters.description: Layout for window arrange: tile, cascade, vertical, or horizontal.

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: Target X coordinate for window move.

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: Target Y coordinate for window move.

parameters.name: command
parameters.description: Shell command (terminal op execute / execute_command).

parameters.name: cwd
parameters.description: Working directory for terminal connect or execute.

parameters.name: sessionId
parameters.description: Terminal session ID alias.

parameters.name: session_id
parameters.description: Upstream terminal session ID alias.

parameters.name: text
parameters.description: Text for terminal type.

parameters.name: timeout
parameters.description: Timeout in seconds (terminal op).

parameters.name: timeoutSeconds
parameters.description: Alias for timeout (terminal op).

actions.descriptionCompressed: Single DESKTOP action; op=file|window|terminal dispatches to the matching computer-use op (screenshot/ocr/detect_elements reserved).

exampleCalls.user: Use DESKTOP with the provided parameters.

actions.name: DISCORD_SETUP_CREDENTIALS
actions.description: Start Discord credential setup or account pairing. Guides the user through setting up API credentials for supported third-party services, validates them when possible, and stores them securely.

actions.name: EDIT
actions.description: Replace text in an existing file. Default behavior requires `old_string` to match exactly once; pass `replace_all=true` to substitute every occurrence. The file must have been READ in this session, must still match its recorded mtime, and the new content cannot introduce a detected secret pattern.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the file to edit.

parameters.name: old_string
parameters.description: Exact substring to replace.

parameters.name: new_string
parameters.description: Replacement text. Must differ from old_string.

parameters.name: replace_all
parameters.description: If true, replace every occurrence; otherwise require exactly one match.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Replace exact-match text in a file (single match by default; pass replace_all for multiple).

exampleCalls.user: Use EDIT with the provided parameters.

actions.name: ENTER_WORKTREE
actions.description: Create a git worktree for the current repo and switch the session into it. The new worktree path becomes the session cwd and a sandbox root, so subsequent file operations land there until EXIT_WORKTREE pops it. Use to isolate a parallel branch of work without disturbing the main checkout.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Optional worktree branch/dir name. Defaults to a random auto-* identifier.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Optional absolute worktree directory. Must lie within sandbox roots. Defaults to a per-call directory under the OS temp dir.

parameters.name: base
parameters.description: Optional base ref for the new worktree (default HEAD).

actions.descriptionCompressed: Create and switch into a git worktree for parallel work.

exampleCalls.user: Use ENTER_WORKTREE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: EXIT_WORKTREE
actions.description: Pop the most recent ENTER_WORKTREE: restore the previous session cwd, drop the added sandbox root, and (with cleanup=true) run `git worktree remove --force` to delete the worktree directory.

parameters.name: cleanup
parameters.description: If true, also `git worktree remove --force` the popped worktree directory.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Exit current worktree, restore previous cwd, optionally git worktree remove --force.

exampleCalls.user: Use EXIT_WORKTREE with the provided parameters.

actions.description: File operations: read, write, or edit a file at an absolute path.

actions.name: GLOB
actions.description: Find files matching a glob pattern (e.g. '**/*.ts'). Returns up to 100 absolute paths sorted by mtime descending. Excludes VCS, build, and dependency directories. Use this instead of BASH for file discovery.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Glob pattern relative to the search root (e.g. '**/*.ts').

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute path of the directory to search. Defaults to the session cwd.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Find files by glob (e.g. '**/*.ts'); returns absolute paths sorted by mtime.

exampleCalls.user: Use GLOB with the provided parameters.

actions.name: GREP
actions.description: Search file contents using ripgrep (a fast regex search). Returns matching files, counts, or line content. Always excludes VCS directories. Use this instead of BASH for content search.

parameters.name: pattern
parameters.description: Regex pattern to search for.

parameters.name: path
parameters.description: Absolute path to a file or directory to search. Defaults to the session cwd.

parameters.name: glob
parameters.description: Optional glob filter passed to ripgrep -g (e.g. '*.ts').

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Optional ripgrep file type passed via -t (e.g. 'js', 'py').

parameters.name: output_mode
parameters.description: How to render matches: 'content' returns matching lines, 'files_with_matches' returns file paths, 'count' returns per-file counts. Defaults to 'files_with_matches'.

parameters.name: -A
parameters.description: Lines of context to show after each match (content mode).

parameters.name: -B
parameters.description: Lines of context to show before each match (content mode).

parameters.name: -C
parameters.description: Lines of context around each match (content mode).

parameters.name: case_insensitive
parameters.description: Match case-insensitively (alias of -i).

parameters.name: multiline
parameters.description: Enable multiline matching (the pattern can span newlines).

parameters.name: head_limit
parameters.description: Truncate output to the first N lines. 0 means unlimited. Defaults to CODING_TOOLS_GREP_HEAD_LIMIT or 250.

parameters.name: show_line_numbers
parameters.description: Show 1-based line numbers in content mode.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Ripgrep wrapper: regex search across files, returns matches/counts/files.

exampleCalls.user: Use GREP with the provided parameters.

actions.name: LINEAR
actions.description: Manage Linear issues, comments, and activity. Operations: create_issue, get_issue, update_issue, delete_issue, create_comment, update_comment, delete_comment, list_comments, get_activity, clear_activity, search_issues. The op is inferred from the message text when not explicitly provided.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Operation to perform. One of: create_issue, get_issue, update_issue, delete_issue, create_comment, update_comment, delete_comment, list_comments, get_activity, clear_activity, search_issues. Inferred from message text when omitted.

parameters.name: range

parameters.name: tokenA
parameters.description: First token filter or deposit token.

parameters.name: tokenB
parameters.description: Second token filter or deposit token.

parameters.name: chainId
parameters.description: Optional numeric EVM chain id.

parameters.name: slippageBps

actions.name: LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS

Use only for website/app blocking status; do not use for inbox blockers, message priority, morning/night briefs, operating pictures, end-of-day reviews, or general executive-assistant triage.

parameters.description: Must be true before queue changes, downloads, or playlist mutations.

exampleCalls.user: Use PLAYBACK with the provided parameters.

actions.name: READ
actions.description: Read the contents of a file at an absolute path. Returns numbered lines, capped by a per-call line limit and a per-file byte limit. Use offset/limit to paginate through large files. Required before WRITE/EDIT can mutate an existing file.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the file to read.

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: Zero-based line offset to start reading from.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max number of lines to return.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Read a file by absolute path; returns numbered lines (offset/limit supported).

exampleCalls.user: Use READ with the provided parameters.

actions.name: READING
actions.description: Mystical reading router. Set type to tarot, astrology, or iching, and subaction to start (begin a new reading), followup (reveal the next element), or deepen (more interpretation for the most-recent element).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Reading type: tarot, astrology, or iching.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Subaction: start, followup, or deepen.

parameters.name: question
parameters.description: Optional question or focus for the reading.

parameters.name: context
parameters.description: Optional additional context (e.g., birth data hint for astrology).

actions.descriptionCompressed: Mystical readings: tarot, astrology, iching; subactions: start, followup, deepen.

exampleCalls.user: Use READING with the provided parameters.

actions.name: RELEASE_BLOCK
actions.description: Release an active website block rule. Requires confirmed:true.

parameters.name: ruleId
parameters.description: ID of the block rule to release.

parameters.name: confirmed
parameters.description: Must be true to release. Prevents accidental unblocking.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Optional reason for release, stored on the rule.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Release a website block rule; requires confirmation.

exampleCalls.user: Use RELEASE_BLOCK with the provided parameters.

actions.name: RS_2004
actions.description: Drive the 2004scape game agent. Choose one op (walk_to, chop, mine, fish, burn, cook, fletch, craft, smith, drop, pickup, equip, unequip, use, use_on_item, use_on_object, open, close, deposit, withdraw, buy, sell, attack, cast_spell, set_style, eat, talk, navigate_dialog, interact_object, open_door, pickpocket). For open/close, set target='bank' or target='shop' (or include npc to imply shop). Per-op fields go in params.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Operation to run.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional JSON object containing the fields required by the chosen op.

actions.descriptionCompressed: rs_2004 ops (walk_to, skills, inventory, bank, shop, combat, interact)

exampleCalls.user: Use RS_2004 with the provided parameters.

actions.name: SCAPE
actions.description: Drive the 'scape (xRSPS) game agent. Pick one op: walk_to (x,z,run?), attack (npcId), chat_public (message), eat (item?), drop (item), set_goal (title,notes?), complete_goal (status?,goalId?,notes?), remember (notes,kind?,weight?). Returns success and a short status message; the autonomous loop already handles its own dispatch — this is the planner-facing surface.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Operation to run.

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: Optional JSON object containing the fields required by the chosen op.

actions.descriptionCompressed: scape ops: walk_to|attack|chat_public|eat|drop|set_goal|complete_goal|remember

exampleCalls.user: Use SCAPE with the provided parameters.

parameters.name: subaction
parameters.description: Optional. summary or inspect.

parameters.name: timezone
parameters.description: Optional IANA timezone override.

actions.descriptionCompressed: passive schedule inference activity+screen-time+health: summary | inspect(sleep meals evidence-windows)

parameters.description: Maximum number of issues to return.

actions.descriptionCompressed: search issue Linear w/ various filter

actions.name: SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD
actions.description: Set a recurring follow-up cadence threshold (in days) for a specific contact.

parameters.name: contactId
parameters.description: UUID of the contact. Preferred when known.

parameters.name: contactName
parameters.description: Human-readable contact name. Must be unambiguous across stored contacts.

parameters.name: thresholdDays
parameters.description: Number of days after last contact before this contact is considered overdue.

exampleCalls.user: Use SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD with the provided parameters.

actions.name: SHOPIFY
actions.description: Manage a Shopify store. Operations: search (read-only catalog browsing across products, orders, and customers), products (CRUD on products), inventory (stock adjustments), orders (list/update orders), customers (CRUD on customers). Op is inferred from the message text when not explicitly provided.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Operation to perform. One of: search, products, inventory, orders, customers. Inferred from message text when omitted.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Search term for op=search.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Search scope for op=search: all, products, orders, or customers.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum results per searched Shopify category.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Shopify: search, products, inventory, orders, customers.

exampleCalls.user: Use SHOPIFY with the provided parameters.

actions.name: SKILL
actions.description: Manage skill catalog. Operations: search (browse available skills), details (info about a specific skill), sync (refresh catalog from registry), toggle (enable/disable installed skill), install (install from registry), uninstall (remove non-bundled skill). For invoking an enabled skill, use USE_SKILL instead.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Operation to perform. One of: search, details, sync, toggle, install, uninstall. Inferred from message text when omitted.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Skill catalog: search, details, sync, toggle, install, uninstall.

exampleCalls.user: Use SKILL with the provided parameters.

actions.name: TAILSCALE
actions.description: Tailscale tunnel router. Operations: start (open tunnel for a local port), stop (close active tunnel). Status reads come from the tailscaleStatus provider.

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Tunnel operation. One of: start, stop.

parameters.name: port
parameters.description: Local port to expose when op is start.

parameters.name: accountId
parameters.description: Optional Tailscale account id from TAILSCALE_ACCOUNTS. Defaults to TAILSCALE_DEFAULT_ACCOUNT_ID or legacy settings.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Tailscale: start tunnel, stop tunnel.

exampleCalls.user: Use TAILSCALE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: TODO
actions.description: Manage the user's todo list. Op-based dispatch — provide an `op` parameter:

parameters.name: op
parameters.description: Operation: write, create, update, complete, cancel, delete, list, clear.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Todo id (update/complete/cancel/delete).

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Imperative form, e.g. 'Add tests' (create/update).

parameters.name: activeForm
parameters.description: Present-continuous form, e.g. 'Adding tests' (create/update).

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: pending | in_progress | completed | cancelled.

parameters.name: parentTodoId
parameters.description: Parent todo id for sub-tasks (create/update).

parameters.name: todos
parameters.description: Array of {id?, content, status, activeForm?} for op=write. Replaces the user's list for this conversation.

parameters.name: includeCompleted
parameters.description: Include completed/cancelled todos in op=list output.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max rows to return for op=list.

actions.descriptionCompressed: todo manage list; op: write|create|update|complete|cancel|delete|list|clear; user-scoped (entityId).

exampleCalls.user: Use TODO with the provided parameters.

actions.name: UPDATE_LINEAR_ISSUE
actions.description: Update an existing Linear issue (title, priority, assignee, status, labels, team)

parameters.name: issueId
parameters.description: Linear issue id or identifier to update.

actions.descriptionCompressed: update Linear issue

exampleCalls.user: Use UPDATE_LINEAR_ISSUE with the provided parameters.

actions.name: USE_SKILL
actions.description: Invoke an enabled skill by slug. The skill's instructions or script run and the result returns to the conversation.
actions.descriptionCompressed: Invoke an enabled skill by slug.

actions.name: WEB_FETCH
actions.description: Fetch a single http(s) URL and return its body as text. HTML responses are stripped of tags and collapsed to plain text. Body is capped at 5MB; text is capped at 50000 chars. Loopback addresses (localhost, 127.0.0.1, 0.0.0.0, ::1, 169.254.*) are blocked by default; set CODING_TOOLS_WEB_FETCH_ALLOW_LOOPBACK=1 to permit them. Use for reading documentation, blog posts, or pasting in a URL the user referenced.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Absolute http:// or https:// URL to fetch.

parameters.name: prompt
parameters.description: Optional summary/extraction instruction. Echoed verbatim in the result text; no LLM is run on the body in v1.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Fetch http(s) URL and return body as plain text (HTML stripped, capped).

exampleCalls.user: Use WEB_FETCH with the provided parameters.

actions.name: WRITE
actions.description: Write a file at an absolute path, replacing any existing contents. The file's parent directory is created if missing. Existing files must have been READ in this session first; otherwise the write is rejected to avoid clobbering external edits.

parameters.name: file_path
parameters.description: Absolute path to the file to write.

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: Full new file contents.

actions.descriptionCompressed: Write a file at an absolute path (creates parents; rejects if existing file was not READ first).

exampleCalls.user: Use WRITE with the provided parameters.
